current_provider: DeepSeek as current production implementation; provider-neutral framework remains the product positioning
current_model: deepseek-chat in code; priced as DeepSeek official current compatibility target deepseek-v4-flash non-thinking mode

current_enforced_normal_path.why_not_linear_with_users: Current shared platform request/token limits are hit before 5/50/100 users can each receive 40 frontend tasks per day.

uncapped_demand_pressure.meaning: Future expansion or relaxed-limit planning cost if high-active user demand is fully served; not currently achievable under enforced production limits.

failure_or_concurrency_escape_risk.limit_check_timing: D1 usage is checked before upstream call; usage is recorded after upstream returns.
failure_or_concurrency_escape_risk.failed_or_timeout_recording: If upstream throws or times out before a response is parsed, recordUsage is not called in the Worker catch path.
failure_or_concurrency_escape_risk.concurrent_requests: Concurrent requests may pass checks against the same previous D1 total before any request records usage.

main_cost_driver: Under current enforced normal path, DAILY_TOKEN_LIMIT is the bottleneck for the 8000 input + 2048 output token assumption. Under uncapped demand pressure, request volume multiplied by upstream calls, output token ceiling, retry multiplier and provider unit price drive cost.
critical_findings: Original 5/50/100 demand-pressure numbers are not current production-limit worst-case costs.
Current enforced normal path is capped by shared daily request/token limits and does not scale linearly with registered users.
Wallet safety remains unsafe because failure/concurrency escape paths are not fail-closed and no monthly amount circuit breaker exists.
No real user usage data exists; all 5/50/100 results remain planning scenarios.
next_action: Wait for product owner re-acceptance of the corrected survival cost audit. Do not implement platform monthly total spend cap or automatic circuit breaker without approval.
